query: Impact of quantum computing on modern cryptography
executive_summary: Quantum computing poses a significant future risk to current asymmetric cryptographic standards like RSA and ECC, primarily due to Shor's algorithm.

technical_breakdown.text: Shor's algorithm can factor large integers and compute discrete logarithms in polynomial time, unlike classical algorithms which take exponential time. This breaks RSA and ECC.
technical_breakdown.evidence: Doc1 section 4 details exponential speedup using QFT.

pros: Transition to PQC (Post-Quantum Cryptography) encourages cryptographic agility.
cons: PQC algorithms often have larger key sizes and slower signature verification.

sources.snippet: Shor's algorithm achieves exponential speedup over classical factoring...

claims.text: RSA-2048 will be broken by a sufficiently large quantum computer.
